name: heydev
description: Real-time status indicators for AI CLI sessions (Claude Code, Codex, Copilot) in VS Code terminal tabs, with smart notifications and quick reply

commands.title: HeyDev: Setup AI CLI Integration (Claude, Codex)

heydev.showNotifications.description: Show VS Code notifications when your AI agent (e.g. Claude Code) has been waiting for your input

heydev.notificationDelaySeconds.description: Seconds to wait before showing a notification (0 = immediate). Notification is cancelled if the AI agent starts working again.

heydev.workingEmoji.description: Emoji shown in terminal tab when the AI CLI is working

heydev.waitingEmoji.description: Emoji shown in terminal tab when the AI CLI is waiting for input

walkthroughs.title: Getting Started with HeyDev
walkthroughs.description: Set up live AI CLI status indicators in your terminal tabs

steps.title: Welcome to HeyDev
steps.description:
HeyDev shows live status indicators in your terminal tabs when running AI CLI sessions.

**⚡ Working** — your AI agent is using tools

**👀 Waiting** — your AI agent needs your input

Plus smart notifications and Quick Reply.

media.altText: Terminal tabs showing Working and Waiting states

steps.title: Install jq (required)
steps.description:
HeyDev needs ``jq`` to parse JSON from AI CLI sessions.

**macOS:** ``brew install jq``

**Linux:** ``sudo apt-get install jq``

Verify: ``jq --version``

steps.title: Run Setup
steps.description:
One command configures everything — hooks, state directory, and terminal settings.

[Run Setup](command:heydev.setup)

steps.title: Restart your AI CLI sessions
steps.description:
Close and reopen your Claude Code or Codex terminal sessions for hooks to take effect.

Once restarted, your terminal tabs will show live status indicators like:

``⚡ Claude [a1b2] - Working``

media.altText: Smart notification with Focus Terminal and Quick Reply

steps.title: Customize your experience
steps.description:
Tweak notification delay, emojis, and more in settings.

[Open HeyDev Settings](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%5B%22heydev%22%5D)
